zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Seymour於1610年9月9日出生在英國戴文貝里 波梅洛易時，他的父親Edward Beauchamp Seymour24歲，母親Dorothy Killigrew30歲。他於1630年與Anne Portman在英國英格蘭維爾爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1688年12月4日在他的家鄉享年78歲，埋葬在英國戴文貝里 波梅洛易。

nameMeanings.name: Seymour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), and possibly also from Saint-Maur-des-Bois (Manche) and Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine), named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Maur (see   Moore   3). The family of this name who have held the title Duke of Somerset since 1547 are said by some to have originated in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), while others claim they are from Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine). They rose to prominence under the Tudors, when Edward Seymour (died 1552) held a high position of office under Henry VIII, who was married to Edward's sister, Jane. The Norman name has been established in Ireland since at least the 17th century.

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Semar
meanings: Germanized form of French Saint Marc, the name of a Huguenot family that migrated to the Palatinate.
English: variant of   Seymour  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Seymour was born on 9 September 1610, in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England, his father, Edward Beauchamp Seymour, was 24 and his mother, Dorothy Killigrew, was 30. He married Anne Portman in 1630, in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 4 December 1688, in his hometown, at the age of 78, and was buried in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Seymour est né le 9 septembre 1610, à Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Edward Beauchamp Seymour, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Killigrew, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Anne Portman en 1630, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 4 décembre 1688, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Seymour am 9. September 1610 in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward Beauchamp Seymour 24 und seine Mutter Dorothy Killigrew 30. Er hat 1630 in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom Anne Portman geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 4. Dezember 1688 im Alter von 78 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Seymour è nato il il 9 settembre 1610 nel Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England, il suo padre, Edward Beauchamp Seymour, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Dorothy Killigrew, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Anne Portman nel 1630, nel Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia. ja.lifeSummary: Edward Seymourが1610年9月9日にイギリスデボンバーリー ポメロイで生まれた時，父Edward Beauchamp Seymourは24歳，母Dorothy Killigrewは30歳であった。1630年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアでAnne Portmanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1688年12月4日に故郷で78歳で死去し，イギリスデボンバーリー ポメロイにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Seymour(이)가 1610년 9월 9일 영국 데폰 베리 폼로이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Beauchamp Seymour(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Dorothy Killigrew(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1630년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에서 Anne Portman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1688년 12월 4일 그의 고향에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 베리 폼로이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Seymour родился 9 сентября 1610 г. в местности бирри помирои, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Beauchamp Seymour было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Killigrew, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Portman в 1630 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 4 декабря 1688 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности бирри помирои, девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Seymour nació el 9 de septiembre de 1610, en Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Edward Beauchamp Seymour, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Dorothy Killigrew, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Anne Portman en 1630, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 4 de diciembre de 1688, en su pueblo, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultado en Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Inglaterra.
